title: StoryGen
shortDescription: A story-writing and reading platform with an AI co-writing assistant — a side project for sharpening full-stack skills.
fullDescription: StoryGen is a personal side project built to push my React and full-stack skills on something more open-ended than a typical CRUD app. It's a platform for writing and reading multi-chapter stories: authors write in a rich-text editor and can select any passage to get AI-generated plot suggestions — next scenes, dialogue, twists, or subplots, tuned by length and genre — powered by Google's Gemini API. Readers can browse stories by genre, follow a feed, and read chapter by chapter. The app uses Firebase for authentication and data (stories, chapters, users) and Cloudinary for image handling, with animations via GSAP and Framer Motion.
problem: Building only guided, well-specified projects (client work, coursework) doesn't force growth in the same way as an open-ended personal idea — there's no fixed spec to lean on, so every architectural and product decision has to be made from scratch.
solution: A self-directed story platform combining a rich-text authoring experience with an AI writing-assistant feature, giving me room to practice React architecture, third-party API integration (Gemini, Firebase, Cloudinary), and product decisions outside of a client brief.
impact: Built an AI writing-assistant feature that generates plot suggestions from any selected passage, tunable by length, scene type, and genre, using the Gemini API.
Integrated a rich-text story editor (Lexical) supporting multi-chapter stories stored and queried in Firebase/Firestore.
Built genre-based browsing, a story feed, and a chapter-by-chapter reading experience.
Used this project to get hands-on with third-party API integration and product decisions outside of a fixed client spec.
